Each slice is a fresh claude -p session — context is actually wiped.
Show Time joins an existing board when another chat is already running.
Stall alarm fires after 5 minutes with no log progress — keep the tab open to hear it.
autopro off deletes the flag; the in-flight slice still finishes cleanly.
The ledger owns memory. Chat history does not.
Vertical Pac-Man tracks slice progress — dots eaten = slices done.
Open the todo rail (opposite Pac-Man) for an OpenCode-style checklist.
Timers pause when a session stalls and resume on the next heartbeat.
Completion rings a 1950s-style dual-tone alarm in this browser tab.
Lane numbers are Chat 1, Chat 2… assigned when each autopro session registers.
Estimate time uses a rolling average of completed slices this session.
Blocked slices stop the runner — fix the ledger note, then re-arm.
Show Time is localhost-only. No cloud, no shared secrets.
Drag and resize the todo panel — position sticks in localStorage.
High score is slices done across all live Show Time lanes this session.
Between slices you will see a pause tip — they rotate so they stay useful.
Repo path with spaces is fine — the runner quotes every path.
One epic per repo flag file; multiple repos can share one Show Time board.
Check the bottom alarm strip to mute stall or complete tones.
Ship is never automatic — autopro ends at check green, not PR merge.
Token SAVED compares autopro vs an estimated continuous-chat monolith.
tok/s and tok/min show model speed; lines/tok·min is yield efficiency.
Left monitor Pac watches horizontal lanes and locks onto stalls or questions.
Use Comment / Steer in the sidebar to guide the next fresh slice.
Bottom ledges are per-chat — flash means that agent needs your input.
